name: wormhole
description: Evidence-gated planning orchestration for AI coding agents.

interface.longDescription: Wormhole provides a local MCP planning kernel with JSONL state, evidence records, open-question gates, Markdown plan artifacts, durable native context packs, context-pack budget review, reversible optimization records, optimized command execution, live sub-orchestrator control, adapter-free local orchestration runs, graph-first repo indexing, graph artifacts, repo watch sessions, git diff detection, repo activity recording, project contract detection, structured diagnostics, impact-aware verification planning, safety scanning, deterministic semantic fallback search, safe LSP probes, LSP feedback replanning, one-shot project onboarding, native architecture maps, entrypoint flow discovery, blast-radius analysis, task-scoped project context packs, agent-facing routing, next-tool recommendations, mission routes, prepared agent context, durable repo/semantic indexes, process-local LSP sessions, dependency security reports, action admission policy, optimization adapters, required Python runtime for graph and media analysis, native media ingestion, shell hook management, discovery-driven tool generation, learned orchestration policy gates, safe live policy feedback, orchestration policy baseline comparison, reasoning strategy research, printed-tool execution, native tool generation, validated writable tool scaffolds, external agent dispatch, shared agent workspace memory, executable external agent dispatch, Printing Press CLI registration, deterministic conductor scaffolds, durable brevity/minimality policy, model-profile traces, typed artifacts, and static workbench rendering. Codex should use the Wormhole loop for planning and orchestration: start a mission, start a round, run project_onboard when entering a repo, use project_intelligence_snapshot, next_best_tool, mission_route, and agent_context_prepare to choose a curated path before selecting lower-level tools, use architecture_map, entrypoint_flow_discover, blast_radius_analyze, and context_pack_generate to understand large repos natively, use ctx_pack_budget_review and ctx_pack_refresh when context packs overflow or stale records need eviction, use durable indexes and test_impact_analyze_v2 when changes are known, use dependency_security_report and action_policy_review before risky implementation work, use lsp_session_* when an installed language server is needed and lsp_feedback_replan when LSP diagnostics should revise mission scope, use optimization_adapter_* when external optimization adapters are configured, query the repo index before broad grep or raw file reads, gather and record evidence, record reusable context, register tasks when parallel work is useful, use agent_workspace_create, agent_workspace_write, agent_workspace_read, and agent_workspace_merge when concurrent workers need shared state, use optimized command runs and verification_run for large outputs or selected checks, use diagnostics_from_command and diagnostics_query for compiler/test findings, use semantic_index_build and semantic_search for deterministic local fallback search, use media ingestion for PDFs/images, use shell hook planning before shell profile edits, use discovery imports/crawls before generating API tools, use learned policy only after replay-gated activation and baseline comparison, use orchestration_policy_live_feedback for bounded live advice without self-activation, record reasoning traces when plan/critique/revision behavior matters, use local orchestration runs for deterministic task waves and outcomes, verify and run Printing Press CLIs before dispatch, validate and write bounded tool scaffolds when useful, execute configured CLI or HTTP agents when a real external run is required, request the gate, and emit the final plan only after the gate opens.
defaultPrompt: Run this through Wormhole: mission_start, round_start, project_intelligence_snapshot, next_best_tool, mission_route, agent_context_prepare, repo_index_query before broad grep, record evidence, resolve questions, gate_request, then emit_plan.

Use Wormhole orchestration for this feature: record context packs, refresh pack budgets when they overflow, create agent workspaces for shared concurrent state, register tasks, plan local orchestration waves, and use verified external agents or printed CLIs only when needed.
Produce a cited Wormhole implementation plan and do not emit it until the evidence gate opens.
